# 🎛️ Meta & Orchestration

> Orchestrators and coordinators that compose, route, and manage other agents.

| Agent | What it does | Model | Tags |

| [`agent-orchestrator`](agent-orchestrator.md) | The default entry point for any request that spans multiple specialties. | 🔴 opus | orchestration, routing, coordination, multi-agent |
| [`agent-organizer`](agent-organizer.md) | The team-assembler. | 🔴 opus | agent-selection, team-assembly, routing, capability-matching |
| [`chief-of-staff`](chief-of-staff.md) | The executive coordinator for a founder or leader. | 🔴 opus | executive, triage, prioritization, routing |
| [`context-manager`](context-manager.md) | The shared-memory keeper for a multi-agent run. | 🟡 sonnet | context, memory, summarization, handoff |
| [`knowledge-synthesizer`](knowledge-synthesizer.md) | The fan-in step that merges outputs from many agents or sources into one coherent, de-duplicated, source-attributed answer. | 🟡 sonnet | synthesis, deduplication, source-attribution, reconciliation |
| [`multi-agent-coordinator`](multi-agent-coordinator.md) | The runtime conductor for many agents working at once. | 🔴 opus | coordination, concurrency, dependency-graph, parallelism |
| [`task-decomposer`](task-decomposer.md) | The planner that breaks a big, fuzzy goal into an ordered, dependency-aware set of well-scoped subtasks. | 🟡 sonnet | decomposition, planning, dependencies, task-graph |
| [`tech-lead-orchestrator`](tech-lead-orchestrator.md) | The technical delivery lead for work that spans multiple engineering agents. | 🔴 opus | engineering, delivery, sequencing, code-review |
| [`workflow-coordinator`](workflow-coordinator.md) | The executor of a defined, multi-step workflow. | 🔴 opus | workflow, phase-gates, handoff-contracts, pipeline |
